title: Build local API server bridging bd CLI to React app
description: The browser can't call bd CLI directly. A small Express server wraps bd --json commands and serves them over HTTP. Vite proxies /api/* to it during dev.
design: server/index.js: Express app with GET /api/beads/ready, /api/beads/list, /api/beads/show/:id. Each route runs bd with --json via child_process.execSync. Add proxy to vite.config.js. Add dev:full script to package.json running both servers concurrently.
close_reason: Express server in server/index.js wraps bd --json for /api/beads/ready|list|show/:id|stats. Vite proxies /api to port 3001. npm start runs both concurrently.

title: Google Calendar OAuth integration — replace mock data
description: The calendar section shows hardcoded mock events. Real calendar data is the highest-value feature not yet built — it makes recommendations actually useful by knowing what time is already committed today. Requires Google Calendar API with browser-side OAuth (no backend needed for a personal tool).
design: 1. Google Cloud Console: create project, enable Calendar API, create OAuth 2.0 credentials (Web App type). Authorized JS origins: http://localhost:5173 and https://life-organizer-mo.netlify.app. Authorized redirect URIs: same. Copy client ID. 2. Add VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID to .env.local (gitignored) and to Netlify env vars. 3. Load Google Identity Services in index.html: <script src='https://accounts.google.com/gsi/client'>. 4. Create useGoogleCalendar hook: tokenClient = google.accounts.oauth2.initTokenClient({client_id, scope:'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.readonly', callback}). On mount if no token, call requestAccessToken(). Store token in sessionStorage (not localStorage — expires). 5. Fetch events: GET https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/primary/events?timeMin=now&timeMax=+7days&singleEvents=true&orderBy=startTime. 6. Normalise to {id, title, start, end, date, type} shape matching existing CalendarContent component. Replace MOCK_CALENDAR. 7. Handle token expiry: re-request on 401.

title: Beads write-back: claim and close from UI via local API
description: Beads tasks are currently read-only in the UI. The accordion shows 'bd close <id>' as a text hint but cannot execute it. Since the local Express server is already running when the app is used locally, claim and close should be possible directly from the UI for that workflow.
design: Server: add POST /api/beads/claim/:id (runs: bd update <id> --status=in_progress) and POST /api/beads/close/:id with JSON body {reason} (runs: bd close <id> --reason='<reason>'). Both run in ~/beads-global with shell zsh. Return {ok, error}. UI: in BeadsTaskRow expanded panel, show Claim button when task status is pending, Close button always. Close triggers an inline reason input (text field + confirm/cancel) before POSTing. Both buttons disabled with tooltip 'server offline' when /api/health fails. On success: refresh beads list. On failure: show inline error. Optimistic update optional — conservative approach (wait for refresh) is safer.

title: Connect Netlify site to GitHub for auto-deploy on push
description: Currently deploying manually via 'netlify deploy --prod'. The Netlify site is created and linked to the repo via API but the GitHub App webhook that triggers builds on git push has not been installed. Every deploy requires a manual CLI command.
design: Manual step required in Netlify UI — cannot be done via CLI alone. Navigate to: app.netlify.com/projects/life-organizer-mo → Site configuration → Build & deploy → Continuous deployment → Connect to GitHub. Select mofro/life-organizer, branch: main, build command: npm run build, publish dir: dist. Verify by pushing a trivial change (e.g. comment) and watching the Netlify dashboard build log. After setup, remove manual netlify deploy from workflow docs.

title: Debug and harden local API server startup
description: User experienced Beads section showing 'server offline' when running npm start. Root cause unclear — may be bd not in PATH when npm spawns child process, server crash on startup, or user ran 'npm start dev' (wrong command). Need diagnostics and hardening so failures are obvious, not silent.
design: 1. Add GET /api/health endpoint that returns {ok:true, bdPath, bdgDir, version}. 2. On server startup, call bd --version and log result — fail loudly if not found. 3. Validate ~/beads-global exists on boot; print clear error if missing. 4. Add npm run check script that validates all prereqs before starting. 5. Update README with exact command (npm start, not npm start dev) and troubleshooting section. 6. Consider wrapping concurrently output with prefixes so server vs vite logs are distinguishable.
close_reason: Implemented all 6 items from design: /api/health endpoint, startup validation (bd PATH + beads-global dir check with process.exit(1)), npm run check script, concurrently prefixes, README troubleshooting section

title: Replace mock Beads data with real bd CLI data in dashboard
description: Dashboard currently shows only manually-added tasks. Replace with real Beads issues fetched from the local API server, displayed alongside manual tasks.
design: New useBeadsTasks hook fetches /api/beads/ready on mount and refresh. Beads tasks rendered in their own section above manual tasks. Source badge shows 'beads' with link to bd show output. Include issue ID, title, priority, status, blocked_by count.
close_reason: useBeadsTasks hook fetches /api/beads/ready, normalises to task model. BeadsTaskRow component shows issue with indigo badge. Recommendations draw from both manual + Beads tasks.

title: Test artifact in Claude.ai
description: Deploy completed artifact to Claude.ai and verify all features work in that environment.
design: Test: form fields keep focus, tasks persist after refresh, recommendations appear, no console errors, mobile layout works.
close_reason: Running locally at localhost:5173 via Vite. Tested HTTP 200. Full dev environment preferable to Claude.ai artifact for local file access in future phases.

title: Implement window.storage persistence
description: Tasks must persist across browser refreshes. Claude.ai artifact environment provides window.storage API.
design: On task add/update/delete: window.storage.set('tasks', JSON.stringify(tasks)). On mount: window.storage.get('tasks').then(...). Handle missing/corrupt data gracefully.
close_reason: storageLoad/storageSave helpers try window.storage first, fall back to localStorage. Saves on every tasks change after initial load.

title: Build task dashboard UI
description: Core UI components for the Life Organizer: Dashboard, TaskList, QuickStats, RecommendationCards. Must look good and be functional.
design: Tailwind CSS styling. Components: Dashboard (layout), TaskForm (uncontrolled), TaskList (sortable), QuickStats (counts by category/priority), RecommendationCards (top 3 suggested tasks).
close_reason: Built Dashboard, QuickStats, RecommendationCards, TaskForm, TaskRow, TaskList, CalendarSection components with Tailwind.

title: Fix form input focus issues
description: Forms lose focus when using controlled inputs with object spread in onChange. Needs to work in Claude.ai artifact environment.
design: Use FormData API with uncontrolled inputs and formRef. No object spread in onChange handlers. Call formRef.current.reset() after submit.
close_reason: Used FormData API with formRef + uncontrolled inputs. form.reset() after submit. No onChange state handlers.

title: Beads close with inline reason prompt in accordion
description: bd close requires a reason string. The close button in the accordion (once Beads write-back is built) should prompt for a reason inline before submitting — not a modal, not a default empty string.
design: When Close is clicked: replace the button with an inline form — a single text input (placeholder: 'Reason for closing…') and two small buttons: ✓ confirm and × cancel. On confirm: POST /api/beads/close/:id with {reason: inputValue}. Input should auto-focus on appear. Enter key confirms, Escape cancels. Show a spinner on the button while the request is in flight. On success: remove task from beads list (optimistic) and show a brief 'Closed' toast. On error: restore the Close button and show inline error text. Depends on: Beads write-back issue.

title: PWA push notifications for tasks with approaching deadlines
description: Tasks with imminent deadlines have no proactive alert. PWA supports browser push notifications via the Notifications API — no server-side storage needed, fires from the client on app open/focus. This gives useful alerts without requiring Supabase or a backend.
design: 1. On app first load, call Notification.requestPermission() — only if not already granted/denied. 2. Create checkUrgentTasks(tasks) function: finds tasks where deadline is within 24h and status !== completed. 3. For each urgent task, check localStorage key lo-notified-<id>-<YYYY-MM-DD> — if not set today, fire new Notification('Life Organizer', {body: task.title + ' is due today', icon: '/icon-192.png'}). Set the localStorage key to prevent repeat firing. 4. Call checkUrgentTasks on mount and on window focus event. 5. Add notification toggle in a minimal Settings panel (collapsible Section at bottom of app). Store preference in localStorage as lo-notifications-enabled. 6. Note: this only fires when app is open/focused. True background notifications require a push server — deferred to later.

title: Add search across manual tasks
description: No way to find a specific task as the list grows. Need real-time text search filtering the task list. Should work alongside existing status filters (active/all/etc).
design: Add searchQuery state (string) to main LifeOrganizer component. Pass to TaskList alongside filter. In TaskList filter logic, apply search after status filter: tasks.filter(t => matchesStatus(t) && matchesSearch(t, q)). matchesSearch checks t.title, t.category, t.source case-insensitively. Add search input to TaskList header row — magnifying glass icon on left, clear (×) button appears when non-empty. Debounce input 150ms via useRef setTimeout. No external library needed.

title: Mobile layout testing and polish
description: Verify the app is usable on real mobile devices (iOS Safari, Android Chrome). The grid and flex layouts are designed to be responsive but real-device testing will surface issues that desktop DevTools miss.
design: Known responsive: two-column sections stack to single-col at sm breakpoint, badge rows wrap, stats grid is 2x2 on mobile. Watch for: tap target sizes on status dropdowns and delete buttons, form field zoom-in on iOS (font-size < 16px triggers), overflowing text in task titles on narrow screens, bottom nav bar overlap on iOS Safari.

title: Implement basic recommendation engine
description: Algorithm that suggests which tasks to work on next based on deadlines, time available, and priority.
design: Three categories: urgent (deadline < 3 days), quick wins (timeRequired < 1h), big blocks (timeRequired >= 3h). Score and rank tasks. Return top 3 recommendations with reason.
close_reason: Scoring algorithm: deadline urgency (+100/60/30), priority boost (+40/20/5), quick wins <60m (+25), focus block fit (+20). Returns top 3.

title: Add calendar event display (mock)
description: Display upcoming calendar events alongside tasks so recommendations can account for scheduled time.
design: Static mock events array with title, start, end, type fields. Styled event cards showing today/tomorrow events. Used by recommendation engine to find free time slots.
close_reason: MOCK_CALENDAR array with today/tomorrow events. CalendarSection component displays them grouped by day.

title: Calendar view modes: day, work-week, month
description: Calendar section currently shows today + tomorrow only. As a planning tool it needs broader time horizons — especially work-week view for sprint planning and month view for deadline awareness. Only worth building once Google Calendar real data is in place.
design: Add calView state: 'day' | 'workweek' | 'month'. Toggle buttons in CalendarSection header. Day: current behaviour (today + tomorrow, time-sorted event list). Work week: Mon-Fri of current week, grouped by day header, events as time-sorted list per day — no pixel-positioned grid needed. Month: 5-row × 7-col grid, each cell shows day number + up to 3 event dots (colored by type), overflow shown as '+N more'. Click a day cell in month view to expand that day's events in a popover. All views use the same normalised event shape from useGoogleCalendar hook. Depends on: Google Calendar OAuth integration.

title: Netlify scheduled function for urgent task email notifications
description: Server-side hourly check for tasks with deadline approaching, sends email digest. Requires Supabase (tasks must be server-accessible — localStorage is client-only). Prerequisite: Supabase integration must be complete.
design: 1. npm install @supabase/supabase-js resend (in netlify/functions/). 2. Create netlify/functions/notify-urgent.mts. In netlify.toml add: [functions.notify-urgent]\nschedule = '0 * * * *'. 3. Function logic: init Supabase with service key (bypasses RLS). Query: SELECT tasks.*, auth.users.email FROM tasks JOIN auth.users ON tasks.user_id=auth.users.id WHERE deadline < NOW()+INTERVAL '24 hours' AND status='pending'. 4. For each result, send via Resend API (resend.com — free tier 3000/month, simple REST API): subject 'Due tomorrow: <title>', plain text body with title + deadline + bd close hint. 5. Track sent notifications in a notifications_sent table (task_id, sent_at) to avoid duplicate emails. 6. Env vars needed in Netlify dashboard: RESEND_API_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY, SUPABASE_URL.

title: Supabase integration for cross-device task storage
description: Manual tasks live in localStorage only — no cross-device sync, no server-side access for scheduled notifications, lost if browser data is cleared. Supabase provides PostgreSQL with Row Level Security, a JS client, and magic-link auth. This unlocks cross-device sync and is a prerequisite for server-side email notifications.
design: 1. Create Supabase project at supabase.com. Run schema: CREATE TABLE tasks (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, user_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users, title TEXT, category TEXT, priority TEXT, time_required INT, deadline TIMESTAMPTZ, status TEXT, source TEXT DEFAULT 'manual', source_url TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()); ALTER TABLE tasks ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY 'own tasks' ON tasks USING (auth.uid()=user_id) WITH CHECK (auth.uid()=user_id). 2. npm install @supabase/supabase-js. 3. Add VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY to .env.local and Netlify env vars. 4. Create useSupabaseTasks hook that mirrors current useState+localStorage pattern but reads/writes Supabase. 5. Auth: supabase.auth.signInWithOtp({email}) magic link — minimal UI (email input + 'Send link' button). 6. Migration path: on first Supabase login, if localStorage has tasks, offer one-click import. 7. Graceful degradation: if not logged in, fall back to localStorage (existing behaviour).

title: Design and generate real PWA icons
description: Current icons are placeholder blue circles generated with Python. PWA install prompt and home screen show these — embarrassing for a tool you actually use daily. Need real branded icons.
design: Design: simple 'LO' monogram or calendar/organizer glyph on blue-600 (#2563eb) background. Keep it readable at 48px (home screen) and sharp at 512px. SVG source is the canonical format. Exports needed: 192x192 PNG (public/icon-192.png), 512x512 PNG (public/icon-512.png), 180x180 PNG (public/apple-touch-icon.png — add to public/ and link in index.html <head>). Tools: Figma (free), Canva, or hand-coded SVG. For apple-touch-icon add: <link rel='apple-touch-icon' href='/apple-touch-icon.png'> to index.html. Also add <meta name='theme-color' content='#2563eb'> if not already present.
